page.title: myasthenia gravis — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for myasthenia gravis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for myasthenia gravis: 174 alterations and 281 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Acupuncture and moxibustion has a good effect on Myasthenia gravis, which is better than conventional Western medicine.
Astragalus has a positive immunomodulatory effect in patients with myasthenia gravia.
Electroacupuncture warming therapy has therapeutic value in the treatment of myasthenia gravis.
Glyconutrient supplementation improves the condition of patients with myasthenia gravis.
Low vitamin D serum levels in a cohort of Myasthenia gravis patients in Argentina.
Molecular and clinical relationship between live-attenuated Japanese encephalitis vaccination and childhood onset myasthenia gravis.
Myasthenia gravis may involve Epstein-Barr infection.
Resistance exercise and creatine supplementation have a therapeutic effect on patients with myasthenia gravis.
Tripterygium wilfordii has a synergistic effect on the efficacy of prednisone in the treatment of myasthenia gravis.
Warming needle moxibustion has therapeutic value in the treatment of myasthenia gravis.
β-glucans reduce LDL cholesterol in patients with myasthenia gravis.
A case of COVID-19 vaccine causing a Myasthenia gravis crisis.
Statin-associated myasthenia gravis has been reported.
Statin-associated myasthenic weakness has been reported.
Astilbin ameliorates experimental autoimmune myasthenia gravis.
Astragaloside IV ameliorates experimental autoimmune myasthenia gravis.
Astragaloside IV protects against autoimmune myasthenia gravis in rats via regulation of mitophagy and apoptosis.
Tetramethylpyrazine attenuates experimental autoimmune myasthenia gravis.
Tripterygium wilfordii attenuates experimentally induced myasthenia gravis in animals.
Astragalus significantlyy reduces autoantibodies in cell cultures from patients wtih myasthenia gravis.
Berberine attenuates experimental autoimmune myasthenia gravis via rebalancing the T cell subsets.
Curcumin (from Turmeric) inhibits autoimmune diseases.
Hepatitis B vaccines have a potential to occasionally trigger the onset of immune diseases in individuals with an underlying genetic or immunological susceptibility.
Melatonin exerts immunoregulatory effects by balancing peripheral effector and regulatory T helper cells in myasthenia gravis.
Statin-associated exacerbation of myasthenia gravis has been reported.

alterations.name: Anti-neuromuscular Junction acetylcholine receptor antibody positivity
EMG: decremental response of compound muscle action potential to repetitive nerve stimulation

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous complete stable remission (CSR) in 10-20% of patients over 10 years; generalised MG rarely resolves without treatment; ocular MG may remain limited in 50-60%
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Thymectomy in non-thymomatous MG at age <65: 50% more likely to achieve CSR at 3 years vs medical therapy (MGTX trial); eculizumab (ravulizumab): 40% reduction in exacerbations in AChR+ MG; efgartigimod alfa (Vyvgart): 67.7% MG-ADL responders (ADAPT trial); zilucoplan: 53% MG-ADL response (RAISE trial)
remission.gap_size: Large — new complement inhibitors and FcRn antagonists represent genuine advances but cost and access are barriers; thymectomy underused in appropriate candidates
remission.barrier_detail: Thymectomy (MGTX trial proven benefit) is delayed or not offered in many patients with non-thymomatous MG; eculizumab ($700K/year) dramatically limits access; efgartigimod and zilucoplan more accessible but still $200K+/year; corticosteroids cause significant morbidity in MG patients managed long-term
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — rozanolixizumab (Rystiggo, FcRn inhibitor) approved; zilucoplan (Zilbrysq, complement C5 inhibitor) 2023; efgartigimod alfa (Vyvgart) 2021; eculizumab 2017; thymectomy MGTX trial 2016; pyridostigmine/corticosteroids standard since 1950s-1960s
remission.outdated_treatments: Plasma exchange or IVIG as long-term maintenance (appropriate for refractory crisis and pre-surgical preparation; not evidence-based for long-term maintenance vs immunosuppressive agents); long-term high-dose prednisolone without steroid-sparing agent; azathioprine without baseline TPMT activity testing (risk of severe leukopenia)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Exercise (previously contraindicated; now established as safe and beneficial for fatigue and function in stable MG); amifampridine (3,4-DAP) for LEMS (related neuromuscular junction disorder); energy conservation techniques; respiratory physiotherapy
remission.frontier_signal: Rozanolixizumab (FcRn inhibitor — subcutaneous, reduces IgG including AChR antibodies); batoclimab (FcRn inhibitor — SC monthly); CAR-T cell therapy depleting pathogenic B cells (German case series extension to MG); anti-FcRn combination approaches; CRISPR for immune tolerance induction
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
